name: screenium

metadata.name: screenium
metadata.summary: Like Selenium, but for your entire screen. Find and interact with text anywhere using OCR.

documentation: # 🎯 Screenium Screenium is like Selenium after discovering there's a whole world outside the browser. Find and click any text on your screen using OCR. That "Accept Cookies" popup for the millionth time? Banish it. That stubborn system dialog that won't go away? Click it. That game that needs grinding? Automate it. ## 🔍 Use Cases - **Auto-Accept Cookies**: Run a background task that automatically clicks "Accept Cookies" popups - **Meeting Assistant**: Auto-join video calls by detecting "Join Meeting" buttons and automatically enable/disable your camera/mic based on meeting names - **Game Automation**: Automate repetitive game tasks by detecting and clicking on in-game text (e.g., "Collect Rewards", "Start Battle") - **System Dialog Monitor**: Handling system or application dialogs, like "Are you sure?" or "Run command," in Cursor based on set rules. - **Multi-Browser Testing**: Perform UI tests on browsers that lack automation APIs or web drivers with no separate web driver installations needed. ## 🔍 Finding Text The most basic way to find text is: ```python from screenium import Text # Simple text matching text = Text("Login") # Finds "Login" anywhere on screen text = Text("LOG") # Matches "LOGIN", "LOGOUT", etc. # Exact matching text = Text("Login", exact_match=True) # Only matches "Login" exactly text = Text("login", case_sensitive=True) # Case-sensitive matching ``` ## 📍 Spatial Relationships You can find text based on its position relative to other text: ```python # Basic relationships Text("Password").below("Username") # Find "Password" below "Username" Text("Cancel").left_of("Submit") # Find "Cancel" left of "Submit" Text("Help").right_of("Back") # Find "Help" right of "Back" Text("Title").above("Content") # Find "Title" above "Content" # Chain relationships Text("Save").below("Options").right_of("Cancel") ``` ### 📐 Aligned Elements For more precise positioning, use `aligned` to require elements to line up: ```python # Elements must be vertically aligned (same x-axis) Text("Email").aligned.below("Username") # Elements must be horizontally aligned (same y-axis) Text("Back").aligned.left_of("Next") ``` ## 🎨 Background Color Matching Find text by its background color: ```python # Match text with specific background colors Text("Error", background_color="red") Text("Success", background_color="green") Text("Info", background_color="#0088ff") # Adjust color matching tolerance (0-100) Text("Warning", background_color="yellow", background_tolerance=50) ``` ## 🖱️ Mouse Actions Interact with matched text: ```python # Basic mouse actions Text("Submit").click() Text("Options").right_click() Text("Link").double_click() Text("Button").mouse_move() # Just move mouse without clicking # Type text Text("Username").click().typewrite("myuser") Text("Password").click().typewrite("mypass") # Special keys Text("Terminal").click().typewrite(["command", "k"]) # Clear terminal ``` ## ⏰ Waiting and Monitoring Handle timing and background monitoring: ```python # Wait for specific duration Text("Loading").wait(2).click() # Wait 2 seconds then click ``` ## 🔍 Inspecting Matches Get detailed information about matches: ```python # Get all matches matches = Text("Button").matches for match in matches: print(f"Found at: ({match['x']}, {match['y']})") print(f"Size: {match['width']}x{match['height']}") print(f"Confidence: {match['confidence']}") ``` ## 🎨 Visual Debugging Draw boxes around matches to debug: ```python # Draw green box for 3 seconds Text("Username").draw(duration=3, color="green") # Chain with other operations Text("Password").below("Username").draw(duration=2, color="red").click() ``` ## 🎯 Tips & Best Practices 1. Start with basic text matching before adding spatial relationships 2. Use `draw()` to visually verify matches 3. Adjust background color tolerance if color matching is too strict/loose 4. Use `aligned` when elements should line up precisely 5. Chain operations for more precise matching ## ⚠️ Limitations - macOS only (uses Apple Vision framework) - Requires macOS Monterey (12.x) or higher - Screen resolution and scaling can affect matching ## 🔐 Required macOS Permissions Screenium needs two key macOS permissions to function. The app running your Python script (e.g., Terminal, VS Code, PyCharm) will need: 1. **Screen Recording** - Open System Settings > Privacy & Security > Screen Recording - Toggle on your terminal app/IDE - Required for: - Finding text on screen using OCR - Background monitoring - Visual debugging with `draw()` 2. **Accessibility** - Open System Settings > Privacy & Security > Accessibility - Toggle on your terminal app/IDE - Required for: - Mouse actions (`click()`, `right_click()`, etc.) - Keyboard input (`typewrite()`, `hotkey()`) > 💡 **Tip**: If running from different apps, each one needs separate permissions. For example, if you run scripts from both Terminal and VS Code, you'll need to grant permissions to both. ---

functions.name: screenium.draw.parse_color
documentation:
Convert various color formats to Color object.

Args:
    color: Can be:
        - Color name (e.g., 'red', 'blue', 'dark green')
        - Hex code (e.g., '#ff0000', '#0088ff')
        - RGB tuple (e.g., (1, 0, 0))
        - Color object

Returns:
    Color object

classes.name: screenium.draw.ShapeDrawer

methods.name: screenium.draw.ShapeDrawer._convert_y_coordinate
documentation:
Convert Y coordinate from top-left to bottom-left origin.

Args:
    y: Y coordinate in top-left coordinate system
    height: Height of the shape being drawn

Returns:
    Y coordinate in bottom-left coordinate system

methods.name: screenium.draw.ShapeDrawer._setup_window
documentation: Set up the overlay window.

methods.name: screenium.draw.ShapeDrawer.add_circle
documentation:
Add a circle to be drawn.

Args:
    x: X coordinate of the circle's top-left corner (from top-left origin)
    y: Y coordinate of the circle's top-left corner (from top-left origin)
    diameter: Diameter of the circle
    color: Color specification, can be:
        - Color name (e.g., 'red', 'blue', 'dark green')
        - Hex code (e.g., '#ff0000', '#0088ff')
        - RGB tuple (e.g., (1, 0, 0))
        - Color object
    line_width: Width of the circle's outline (default: 5.0)

methods.name: screenium.draw.ShapeDrawer.add_rectangle
documentation:
Add a rectangle to be drawn.

Args:
    x: X coordinate of the rectangle's top-left corner (from top-left origin)
    y: Y coordinate of the rectangle's top-left corner (from top-left origin)
    width: Width of the rectangle
    height: Height of the rectangle
    color: Color specification, can be:
        - Color name (e.g., 'red', 'blue', 'dark green')
        - Hex code (e.g., '#ff0000', '#0088ff')
        - RGB tuple (e.g., (1, 0, 0))
        - Color object
    line_width: Width of the rectangle's outline (default: 5.0)

methods.name: screenium.draw.ShapeDrawer.add_image
documentation:
Add an image to be drawn.

Args:
    x: X coordinate of the image's top-left corner (from top-left origin)
    y: Y coordinate of the image's top-left corner (from top-left origin)
    width: Target width (maintains aspect ratio if height is None)
    height: Target height (maintains aspect ratio if width is None)
    percent: Scale image by percentage (e.g., 50 for 50%)
    image_path: Path to the image file

methods.name: screenium.draw.ShapeDrawer.show
documentation: Show all added shapes for the specified duration.
Main class for drawing shapes on screen.

modules.name: screenium.locator
documentation: Screen OCR and text interaction tool using Apple's VisionKit framework.
!/usr/bin/env python3

functions.name: screenium.locator._get_macos_version
documentation: Get the macOS version number.
Check for macOS Monterey or higher

functions.name: screenium.locator._check_monterey_compatibility
documentation: Check if running on macOS Monterey or higher.

functions.name: screenium.locator.get_active_window_geometry
documentation:
Get the geometry of the currently active window.

Returns:
    WindowGeometry: Window position and size
    None: If no active window is found

functions.name: screenium.locator.get_window_geometry_by_app
documentation:
Get the geometry of a window belonging to the specified app.

Args:
    app_name: Name of the application to find

Returns:
    WindowGeometry: Window position and size
    None: If no matching window is found

functions.name: screenium.locator.get_active_window_name
documentation:
Get the name of the currently active (frontmost) window.

Returns:
    str: Name of the active window, or "Unknown" if not found

methods.name: screenium.locator.ScreenCapture.get_current_monitor
documentation:
Get the geometry of the current monitor (where the mouse cursor is).

Returns:
    Dictionary containing monitor geometry:
    - left: X coordinate of the left edge
    - top: Y coordinate of the top edge
    - width: Width of the monitor
    - height: Height of the monitor

classes.name: screenium.locator.Text

methods.name: screenium.locator.Text.__init__
documentation:
Initialize a text locator.

Args:
    text: Text to search for
    image_path: Path to an image file to search in. If None, will capture screen.
    app_name: Restrict search to this application's windows
    case_sensitive: Whether to perform case-sensitive search
    exact_match: Whether to require exact text match (no substrings)
    background_color: Color to match against the text background. Can be:
        - Color name (e.g., 'red', 'blue', 'green')
        - Hex code (e.g., '#ff0000', '#0088ff')
        - RGB tuple (e.g., (1, 0, 0))
        - Color object
    background_tolerance: How much the color can differ (0-100):
        - 0: Exact match only
        - 35: Best for basic colors (default)
        - 50: Very different colors
    recognition_level: OCR recognition level ("fast" or "accurate")
    finder: Optional TextFinder instance to reuse OCR results
    operations: List of operations to perform (for internal use in chaining)

methods.name: screenium.locator.Text._clone_with_operation
documentation: Create a new Text instance with an additional operation.

methods.name: screenium.locator.Text.left_of
documentation: Find this element left of another element.

methods.name: screenium.locator.Text.right_of
documentation: Find this element right of another element.

methods.name: screenium.locator.Text.above
documentation: Find this element above another element.

methods.name: screenium.locator.Text.below
documentation: Find this element below another element.

methods.name: screenium.locator.Text.aligned
documentation: Indicate that the next spatial operation should require alignment.

methods.name: screenium.locator.Text._add_action
documentation: Add an action to the operation chain and execute if not in background mode.

methods.name: screenium.locator.Text.mouse_move
documentation: Move the mouse to the matched text location.

methods.name: screenium.locator.Text.click
documentation: Click on the matched text location.

methods.name: screenium.locator.Text.right_click
documentation: Right click on the matched text location.

methods.name: screenium.locator.Text.double_click
documentation: Double click on the matched text location.

methods.name: screenium.locator.Text.draw
documentation:
Draw rectangles around all matches.

Args:
    duration: How long the shapes should stay on screen (in seconds)
    color: Color of the rectangles (name, hex code, or RGB tuple)
    line_width: Width of the rectangle's outline (default: 5.0)
    padding: Extra space around the text in pixels (default: 5)

Returns:
    self for method chaining

methods.name: screenium.locator.Text.typewrite
documentation:
Type text at the current cursor position.

Args:
    text: String to type or list of key names (e.g. ['a', 'b', 'enter', 'f1'])
    interval: Seconds between each key. If 0, typing is at maximum speed.

Returns:
    self for method chaining

methods.name: screenium.locator.Text.hotkey
documentation:
Press a keyboard hotkey combination (e.g. ctrl+c, ctrl+v).

Args:
    *keys: Variable number of key names (e.g. 'ctrl', 'c' for Ctrl+C)

Returns:
    self for method chaining

methods.name: screenium.locator.Text.wait
documentation:
Wait for the specified number of seconds before continuing the chain.

Args:
    seconds: Number of seconds to wait

Returns:
    self for method chaining

methods.name: screenium.locator.Text.background
documentation:
Monitor text presence using the LocatorManager.

Args:
    every: Time interval in seconds between checks

Returns:
    self for method chaining

methods.name: screenium.locator.Text.stop_background
documentation:
Stop background monitoring if active.

Returns:
    self for method chaining

methods.name: screenium.locator.Text._execute_operations
documentation: Execute all operations in the chain and return the final match.

methods.name: screenium.locator.Text._find_matches
documentation: Find all matches for this text on screen.

methods.name: screenium.locator.Text._filter_by_background_color
documentation:
Filter matches based on background color.

Uses color range analysis to find similar colors in the region and group them
together for more robust background color detection. Color distances are normalized
to a 0-100 scale where 0 is an exact match and 100 is the maximum possible difference.

methods.name: screenium.locator.Text._ensure_locator
documentation: Convert string to Text if needed.

methods.name: screenium.locator.Text._get_match
documentation: Get the best match for this text.

methods.name: screenium.locator.Text._get_middle_point
documentation: Calculate the middle point of a match.

methods.name: screenium.locator.Text._check_spatial_relationship
documentation:
Check if elements satisfy a spatial relationship with optional alignment.

Args:
    a: First element
    b: Second element
    position_check: Whether the basic position requirement is met
    is_vertical: Whether to check vertical (True) or horizontal (False) overlap

methods.name: screenium.locator.Text._is_left_of
documentation: Check if element a is left of element b.

methods.name: screenium.locator.Text._is_right_of
documentation: Check if element a is right of element b.

methods.name: screenium.locator.Text._is_above
documentation: Check if element a is above element b.

methods.name: screenium.locator.Text._is_below
documentation: Check if element a is below element b.

methods.name: screenium.locator.Text._is_aligned_operation
documentation: Check if the current operation should require alignment.

methods.name: screenium.locator.Text._has_vertical_overlap
documentation: Check if two elements have vertical overlap.

methods.name: screenium.locator.Text._has_horizontal_overlap
documentation: Check if two elements have horizontal overlap.

methods.name: screenium.locator.Text.__bool__
documentation: Return True if text is found on screen with all operations in chain.

methods.name: screenium.locator.Text.__str__
documentation: Return string representation of the text locator.

methods.name: screenium.locator.Text.__repr__
documentation: Return detailed string representation of the text locator.

methods.name: screenium.locator.Text.matches
documentation: Return all matches at the current point in the execution chain.

methods.name: screenium.locator.Text.region
documentation:
Get the geometry of the region being used for OCR.

Returns:
    Dictionary containing:
    - left: X coordinate of the left edge
    - top: Y coordinate of the top edge
    - width: Width of the region
    - height: Height of the region
Represents a chainable locator for finding and interacting with text on screen.

classes.name: screenium.locator.WindowGeometry
documentation: Window position and size information.

classes.name: screenium.locator.TextFinder

methods.name: screenium.locator.TextFinder.__init__
documentation:
Initialize TextFinder.

Args:
    app_name: Restrict search to windows of this application
    image_path: Path to an image file to search in. If None, will capture screen.
    recognition_level: OCR recognition level ("fast" or "accurate")

methods.name: screenium.locator.TextFinder._get_dimensions
documentation:
Get dimensions based on whether we're using a file or screen capture.

Returns:
    tuple[int, int]: Width and height as integers

methods.name: screenium.locator.TextFinder.run_ocr
documentation: Run OCR on the current screen and update results.

methods.name: screenium.locator.TextFinder.extract_matches
documentation:
Extract matches for text from OCR results.

Args:
    text: Text to search for. If None or empty string, returns all OCR results.
    case_sensitive: Whether to perform case-sensitive search
    exact_match: Whether to require exact text match (no substrings)

methods.name: screenium.locator.TextFinder.find_text
documentation: Find matches for text, using cache if available.
Finds and validates text elements and their relationships.
